title: How to Transfer Vehicle RC Online — Used Car & Bike Ownership Transfer
meta_description: RC transfer online 2026 — parivahan.gov.in Sarathi portal, NOC for inter-state, documents needed, fees, and inspection process step-by-step.

hero.one_liner: Transfer vehicle Registration Certificate to your name after buying used car/bike — complete online on Parivahan with one RTO visit for inspection

sections.heading: Pre-Transfer Checklist — Before Buying a Used Vehicle
content:
Before you complete the payment for a used vehicle, verify:

1. CHECK VEHICLE STATUS ON parivahan.gov.in:
   - Go to Parivahan → 'Check Hypothecation Status' → enter registration number
   - Verify: Vehicle is NOT hypothecated (no pending loan)
   - If hypothecated (loan active), seller must get NOC from financing bank FIRST — then transfer happens

2.

CHECK FOR PENDING CHALLANS:
   - Go to echallan.parivahan.gov.in → enter registration number
   - Check if there are pending traffic challans/fines
   - Seller is responsible for paying all pending challans — get confirmation or payment receipts

3.

CHECK OWNERSHIP & HISTORY:
   - Get vehicle's ownership history from seller (how many previous owners)
   - Check insurance is transferable (some insurance lapse when ownership changes)
   - Take a test drive and verify chassis number & engine number match RC

4.

GET INSURANCE TRANSFERRED:
   - Contact seller's insurance company — transfer policy to your name
   - OR get a fresh insurance policy from your insurer
   - Insurance MUST be in your name before RC transfer — many RTOs won't process without it

5.

GET VALID PUC CERTIFICATE:
   - Vehicle needs current Pollution Under Control (PUC) certificate
   - If PUC expired, get it renewed before applying for RC transfer
   - PUC is valid for 6 months to 1 year depending on vehicle age

steps.title: Check if NOC is needed (inter-state vs intra-state)
steps.description: Same state: No NOC needed. Different state: Seller must get NOC (No Objection Certificate) from their RTO. The seller applies at their RTO for NOC on parivahan.gov.in. Fee: ₹200-500. Processing: 7-15 days. NOC is valid for 6 months — seller should get it near the time of sale.

steps.title: Seller signs Form 29 and Form 30
steps.description: Form 29: Notice of transfer of ownership. Form 30: Application for registration of transfer of vehicle. Both forms are available at RTO office free or downloadable from parivahan.gov.in. BOTH parties (buyer + seller) must sign these forms. These are crucial documents — get signatures in front of witnesses if possible.

steps.title: Get insurance transferred to your name
steps.description: Contact seller's insurance company with: old insurance policy number, seller's details, and buyer's (your) details. Get a letter from insurer confirming transfer. OR apply for fresh insurance policy from your chosen insurer in your name. Upload this insurance transfer letter/new policy document during online application.

steps.title: Buyer applies online at parivahan.gov.in (Sarathi)
steps.description: Go to parivahan.gov.in → Select your state → Sarathi portal → 'Services' → 'Transfer of Ownership' → Enter vehicle registration number → Fill buyer's (your) details: name, address, Aadhaar, mobile number. This generates an online application with a reference number.

steps.title: Upload documents during online application
steps.description: Upload (as PDF/JPEG, each <500KB): (1) Form 29 (signed by both), (2) Form 30 (signed by both), (3) Original RC book scanned, (4) Buyer's Aadhaar, (5) Seller's Aadhaar, (6) Buyer's address proof (utility bill/bank statement), (7) Seller's address proof, (8) Insurance transfer letter or new policy in buyer's name, (9) Valid PUC certificate, (10) NOC (if inter-state), (11) Any other orignal documents like sale deed or receipt.

steps.title: Pay transfer fee online
steps.description: Intra-state (same state): ₹500-600. Inter-state (different state): ₹500-600 + road tax of destination state. Road tax: ₹5,000-50,000+ depending on vehicle age, type, and engine capacity. Example: 10-year-old sedan, inter-state transfer to state with high tax = ₹15,000 road tax + ₹600 transfer = ₹15,600. Pay via net banking, debit card, or UPI.

steps.title: Book appointment at your RTO for vehicle inspection
steps.description: After online payment, Sarathi portal allows you to book an appointment at your nearest RTO for physical vehicle inspection and document verification. Choose convenient date and time. Some RTOs have online slot booking; some require visiting in person. Most RTOs process inspections within 3-7 days.

steps.title: Visit RTO on appointment day with vehicle and documents
steps.description: Bring: (1) Printed application receipt from Sarathi, (2) Original documents: buyer's Aadhaar, seller's Aadhaar, insurance policy, PUC certificate, (3) Signed Form 29 & 30, (4) NOC (if inter-state), (5) The original RC book (seller hands this to you), (6) Your vehicle for physical inspection.

steps.title: Vehicle inspection at RTO — chassis & engine number verification
steps.description: RTO staff verify: (1) Chassis number on vehicle matches RC and Form 29/30, (2) Engine number matches RC, (3) Vehicle condition is acceptable, (4) No visible damage affecting registration, (5) PUC certificate is valid. Physical inspection takes 30-60 minutes. Both buyer and seller should be present (seller's presence optional if document signatures are clear). After inspection approval, you get a receipt.

steps.title: RC updated to buyer's name — Smart card issued
steps.description: After inspection, RTO processes the transfer in their system. New RC book is prepared with your (buyer's) name as the owner. Smart card RC is printed and mailed to your registered address via post in 7-15 days. Digital RC is available on Parivahan/DigiLocker portal immediately — you can download and use it as proof. Until smart card arrives, use the inspection receipt + digital RC as proof of ownership.

sections.heading: RC Transfer for Special Cases
content: GIFTED VEHICLE: Instead of sale deed, prepare a GIFT DEED on stamp paper (₹50-100). Get notarized.
Gift deed must declare: vehicle details, giver's name, receiver's name, that it's a free gift without any monetary consideration. Otherwise, process is same as sale transfer.

INHERITED VEHICLE: Prepare SUCCESSION CERTIFICATE from civil court OR LEGAL HEIR CERTIFICATE from Gram Panchayat/municipal authority. Attach: death certificate of previous owner + succession certificate.
RTO processes inheritance transfers — can take 30-60 days due to documentation verification. HYPOTHECATED VEHICLE (with active loan): Vehicle is still mortgaged to the financing bank.
Seller must get NOC (No Objection Certificate) from financing bank first — this confirms the loan is closed or will be closed. Then proceed with normal transfer process.
VEHICLE WITH PENDING CHALLANS: All pending traffic challans must be cleared before transfer. Check echallan.parivahan.gov.in for pending challans.
Seller must pay all fines. Once paid, you can proceed with transfer.

sections.heading: Documents Checklist — Buyer & Seller
rows: Should be current (within 3 months) and match Aadhaar
Available at RTO free or download from parivahan.gov.in
Or new policy in buyer's name
Bring to RTO on appointment day
For physical verification of chassis/engine number

faqs.question: Can I drive the vehicle before RC is transferred to my name?
faqs.answer: You should NOT drive without RC in your name. However, practically: (1) Legally, ownership transfer should complete within 7 days of sale. (2) If you're driving before RC transfer, keep the inspection receipt + digital RC (from Sarathi) + sale receipt + insurance in buyer's name. (3) If stopped by police, show these documents as proof you're in the process of transferring ownership. (4) Insurance MUST be in your name — if accident occurs before RC transfer, insurance won't cover if RC is in seller's name. Always prioritize getting RC transferred.

faqs.question: Seller is not cooperating for RC transfer. What's my legal option?
faqs.answer: You need seller's signature on Form 29 & 30 (non-negotiable). If seller refuses after you've paid: (1) Send legal notice via registered post demanding cooperation, (2) File a police complaint about cheating/fraud, (3) Approach consumer court claiming financial loss from non-transfer, (4) As last resort, file civil case for specific performance (court compels seller to sign). ALWAYS get Forms 29 & 30 signed BEFORE making full payment — never pay in full first.

faqs.question: What if RC transfer takes longer than 30 days?
faqs.answer: Typical processing: 7-30 days after inspection. If it's taking longer: (1) Visit RTO in person and inquire, (2) Call RTO's helpline with your reference number, (3) Check Parivahan portal for application status, (4) If RTO isn't responsive, escalate to Vahan Help (parivahan.gov.in helpline). Delays usually happen due to: pending verification, document issues, or server glitches. Follow up proactively.

faqs.question: Vehicle was imported (foreign origin). Any special process?
faqs.answer: Imported vehicles go through customs clearance and get an 'Imported Vehicle Certificate'. If you're buying an imported vehicle: (1) Verify it has completed customs clearance, (2) Verify Bill of Entry number and import certificate are in seller's name, (3) Check if there are any customs duties pending, (4) Standard RC transfer process is same — just note that some RTOs take longer for imported vehicles (may ask for additional verification).

faqs.question: Can I sell/gift the vehicle to someone else before completing RC transfer to my name?
faqs.answer: Technically, you're legally the owner once you have the sale receipt and inspection report. However, for clean title and to avoid complications, complete the RC transfer first before selling to another person. If you sell before transferring RC to yourself, the chain of ownership becomes messy and creates title issues for the final buyer. Always get RC in your name before selling.

faqs.question: Vehicle has run 1,00,000 km. Will RC transfer be rejected?
faqs.answer: No — mileage doesn't affect RC transfer. The vehicle just needs: valid insurance, valid PUC, no pending challans, and chassis/engine numbers matching. Odometer reading is noted in the inspection, but it doesn't block transfer. High mileage only affects resale value and insurance premium (may be higher for old vehicles), not transfer eligibility.

disclaimer: RC transfer process and fees vary by state. Check parivahan.gov.in and your state RTO's specific procedures for exact fees, documents, and processing timelines. Road tax rates vary significantly by state — confirm with your destination state RTO.
